mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases to disrupt oncogenic signaling (includes FLT3 activity)
additional_notes: Approved for CML/ALL; broad kinase incl. FLT3. Range 0.1 nM–1 µM (top 1 µM).

mechanism: Inhibits SRC-family and ABL kinases to suppress proliferation in susceptible AML subsets
additional_notes: Approved; SRC/ABL inhibitor; AML subsets mentioned. Range 0.3 nM–3 µM (top 3 µM).

mechanism: Inhibits MEK in the MAPK pathway to block MAPK-driven proliferation
additional_notes: Approved; MEK inhibitor; MAPK-driven AML subsets. Range 0.3 nM–1 µM (top 1 µM).

mechanism: Inhibits mTOR signaling to reduce growth and survival pathways
additional_notes: Approved; mTOR inhibitor. Range 0.3 nM–3 µM (top 3 µM).

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to alter chromatin/transcription and reduce AML viability
additional_notes: Approved for myeloma; pan-HDAC inhibitor. Range 0.1 nM–300 nM (top 0.3 µM).

mechanism: Inhibits HDACs leading to transcriptional changes and growth inhibition
additional_notes: Approved; HDAC inhibitor. Range 0.03 nM–30 nM (top 30 nM).

mechanism: Weakly inhibits HDAC activity; can promote differentiation/epigenetic modulation at high concentrations
additional_notes: Approved antiseizure; noted often needs high µM–mM. Range 10 µM–5 mM (top 5 mM); ensure osmolarity/pH controls.

mechanism: Activates retinoic acid receptors to induce differentiation programs (classically in APL)
additional_notes: Also referred to as ATRA (in parentheses). Approved APL; sometimes impacts non-APL AML in combinations. Light-sensitive. Range 0.1 nM–10 µM (top 10 µM).

mechanism: Inhibits BCL-2 and BCL-XL to promote apoptosis
additional_notes: Also referred to as ABT-263 (in parentheses). Clinical-stage. Range 0.3 nM–3 µM (top 3 µM).

mechanism: Reported to disrupt lysosomal function/autophagy, inducing cellular stress in AML contexts
additional_notes: Approved antimalarial; AML activity reported in some studies. Range 10 nM–30 µM (top 30 µM).

mechanism: Induces mitochondrial complex I stress and metabolic perturbation (often high in vitro doses)
additional_notes: Approved; noted often requires high µM–mM in vitro. Range 10 µM–10 mM (top 10 mM).

mechanism: Induces oxidative stress and proteostasis disruption leading to cell death
additional_notes: Approved APL; sometimes active beyond APL. Range 10 nM–30 µM (top 30 µM).

mechanism: Induces cellular stress via ALDH/proteasome/NF-κB-related pathways; activity often enhanced by copper
additional_notes: Mentioned as disulfiram (+ copper). Approved for alcohol aversion; copper-dependent activity often stronger. Range 1 nM–10 µM (top 10 µM). If using CuCl2 supplementation: fixed 0.1–1 µM Cu2+ and titrate disulfiram.

mechanism: Reported inhibition of Wnt/STAT3 signaling and mitochondrial uncoupling effects
additional_notes: Approved anthelmintic. Range 10 nM–10 µM (top 10 µM); watch solubility.
